notes: First Levantine hate/abusive dataset; heavily skewed to Lebanese political events
First Tunisian dialect hate/abusive dataset
Widely used benchmark; basis for many later models

dataset_name: OSACT5 / Mubarak et al. abusive dataset
categories: Offensive language, Hate speech, Fine-grained hate
notes: Three subtasks: offensiveness, hate speech, fine-grained hate type; uses emoji cues
Referenced widely as baseline offensive-language corpus
Focused on international news-site comments in dialectal Arabic

dataset_name: Are They Our Brothers? (Religious Hate Speech)
notes: First dedicated Arabic religious-hate dataset; contains duplicate tweets, IAA 81% (hate) / 55% (group)
First Arabic misogyny dataset; basis for ArMI shared task

annotation_scheme: Binary, perspectivist (3 annotators of differing ideology/gender)
notes: Notable for preserving annotator disagreement instead of majority vote
Extends Let-Mi with additional hashtag-scraped tweets
Very large scale; first Jordanian-specific hate speech resource

annotation_scheme: Binary hate/not + 4 hate categories, dialect-labeled per sentence
notes: Explicitly balanced across 5 dialect groups and 4 categories; 90-92% F1 with AraBERT; authors flag Iraqi dialect as future work

categories: Hate speech, Emotion, Offensive content, Fact-checking
notes: Collected Aug-Oct 2020; rich multi-label scheme beyond binary hate

authors_year: Essefar, Ait Baha, El Mahdaouy, El Mekki & Berrada, 2023
annotation_scheme: Binary + fine categories (vulgarity, hostility, contempt, humiliation)
notes: Most widely reused Moroccan Darija toxicity backbone; analyzed emoji impact on detection

dataset_name: Moroccan Darija Offensive Language Dataset v2 (Mendeley)
categories: Offensive language, Hate speech, Hostile intent, Contempt, Humiliation
annotation_scheme: Multi-class (relabeled with Claude 3.5 Sonnet + manual review)
notes: Notably relabeled using an LLM (Claude 3.5 Sonnet) then manually reconciled — relevant precedent for Hayā's own annotation pipeline

platform_source: Merged from all public Arabic hate speech datasets as of Apr 2024
notes: CRITICAL RESOURCE: pre-merged superset of L-HSAB, Let-Mi, MLMA, Albadi 'brothers', OSACT, T-HSAB, Saudi, AraCOVID19-MFH etc., with country metadata; single point of ingestion for a huge share of the field

platform_source: Compiled from multiple standard + dialectal sources
notes: Compiled corpus behind the arHateDetector model, evaluated across 7 datasets
Topic-specific to COVID-19 misinformation/hate overlap

dataset_name: Mubarak et al. Obscene/Offensive News Comments

platform_source: Aggregated from many public hate speech datasets
notes: Not Arabic-specific but a useful cross-lingual harmonization reference/methodology
Arabic portion of the 5-language SemEval shared task

dataset_name: Masader / Masader Plus (catalogue, not a single dataset)
categories: All Arabic NLP task types, incl. ~11+ toxic-language-tagged entries
notes: META-RESOURCE: largest public catalogue of Arabic NLP datasets (600+); should be queried periodically as a discovery pipeline, not treated as a single dataset

categories: Hate speech (cross-lingual catalogue, incl. Arabic entries)
notes: META-RESOURCE: cross-lingual hate speech dataset catalogue that indexes L-HSAB and other Arabic sets alongside global ones

dataset_name: Systematic Review of Arabic Toxic-Language Datasets (source list of 51-54 datasets)
categories: Hate speech, Offensive, Abusive, Toxic language (54 datasets reviewed)

dialects: Gulf, Levantine, Nile Basin, North African, Yemeni, MSA
categories: Toxicity (Hatred, Cussing, Racial, Appearance, Sexual, Violence)

categories: Hate speech, Violence, Terrorism, Safety (9 categories)

categories: Hate speech, Offensive language, Emotion detection

Kaggle-hosted; hybrid Arabic transformer approach

categories: Cyberbullying, Offensive language (sexual, racial, appearance, intelligence)
notes: Distinguishes cyberbullying from general offensive; 5 annotators; includes sexual harassment category
Arabic subset of 540K multilingual dataset; representative random sample (not keyword-biased)

notes: Gender-hate against Saudi female athletes/fans; informed by interviews with 33 Saudi women in sports
Arabic-English code-mixing focus; 120 keyword terms; SGD with TF-IDF 98.21% accuracy
